mechanism_narrative: A lower federal funds rate supports equity valuations by reducing discount rates and easing financial conditions, so a declining FEDFUNDS trend predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next month. The signal is falsified if rate cuts do not coincide with better forward returns than stable or rising-rate periods.
failure_condition: If 20-day forward S&P 500 returns are not higher after month-over-month declines in FEDFUNDS, or if the sign of the relationship turns negative in a rolling 2-year window, reject this hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in unemployment above a short-run threshold signals weakening earnings and demand, which should compress equity prices over the next month. The effect is strongest when UNRATE jumps from its recent level rather than when it is merely elevated.
failure_condition: Reject if threshold-crossing episodes are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases, or if the average forward return after the trigger is indistinguishable from baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous improvement in retail sales and employment indicates broad demand momentum, supporting earnings revisions and higher equity returns over the next quarter. The mechanism requires both consumer spending and payroll growth; either series alone is insufficient.
failure_condition: Reject if joint positive changes in RSAFS and PAYEMS do not outperform single-series signals in predicting 60-day forward returns, or if the joint effect is not positive in a majority of rolling out-of-sample windows.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production signals strengthening real output and improving corporate revenue expectations, which should lift S&P 500 returns over the next month. The signal is most reliable when INDPRO is accelerating rather than merely stable.
failure_condition: If increases in INDPRO fail to precede positive 20-day S&P 500 returns or the sign of the relationship is negative in any sustained 3-year window, reject this hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation is elevated and the policy rate is also high, equity multiples face a double headwind from discount-rate pressure and persistent inflation uncertainty, producing negative 5-day returns. The effect should appear only when CPI and FEDFUNDS are jointly high, not when either is high alone.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL inflation trend > 3% annualized and FEDFUNDS > 4.5%
failure_condition: Reject if high-inflation/high-rate episodes do not produce negative 5-day S&P 500 returns more often than not, or if the interaction term is not materially stronger than either proxy alone.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in housing starts signals weaker construction activity and a coming slowdown in demand-sensitive sectors, which should weigh on equities over the next quarter. The mechanism is directional because housing is a leading real-activity indicator.
failure_condition: Reject if large housing-start declines are followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in a majority of cases, or if the conditional average return is not below the unconditional average.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll gains combined with falling unemployment indicate a strengthening labor market that supports consumption and corporate revenue, producing positive 20-day equity returns. The signal fails when payroll growth is offset by rising unemployment, because that pattern implies poor labor quality rather than genuine improvement.
failure_condition: Reject if combined PAYEMS gains and UNRATE declines do not outperform either series alone in predicting 20-day returns, or if positive combined-labor episodes are followed by negative returns more than 40% of the time.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-recession rebound, rising GDP and retail sales indicate synchronized recovery in output and consumption, which should generate strong positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter. The relationship should be strongest only after an outright contraction or very weak growth phase.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint GDP-and-sales recovery pattern does not produce positive 60-day returns after low-growth periods, or if the signal disappears outside recovery regimes without losing strength inside them.
